mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuation multiples expand.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance with a p-value greater than 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline, or 60-day returns are negative in > 60% of co-occurring expansion months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity utilization nearing peaks, which compresses profit margins and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when INDPRO rises above its 5-year median during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth rates directly correlate with improved corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in quarterly earnings beats.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with GDPC1 revisions with a Rank IC below 0.01, or positive return frequency falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation surpasses 5.0 percent year-over-year, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 5.0 percent threshold, or the signal predictive power vanishes after controlling for rate expectations.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer spending forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 55 percent of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks and manufacturing weakness, triggering risk aversion and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60 percent of instances when INDPRO declines during post-shock recovery periods, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.
